entry.title: Washington Post: 26 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Strikes hit Kyiv; Biden says G-7 will ban Russian gold imports
  • Johnson, Trudeau joke about going shirtless to threaten Putin with ‘our pecs’
  • Biden: G-7 will ban Russian gold imports to deny Putin revenue for war in Ukraine
  • Embattled Scholz, hosting first major summit, tries to prove mettle
  • A Russian musician mounts a modest antiwar protest and pays the price
  • Monkeypox cases surge as WHO stops short of declaring a global emergency
  • Russia will soon exhaust its combat capabilities, Western assessments predict
  • Two killed in attack on Oslo LGBTQ club; Norway raises terror alert
  • U.S. abortion decision draws cheers, horror abroad
  • Ukraine accuses Russia of launching missiles from Belarusian airspace
  • Va. couple who went missing during sailing trip found safe, Coast Guard says
  • Destruction everywhere, help scarce after Afghanistan quake
  • Iran launches rocket into space as nuclear talks to resume
  • Live updates | US sees 'profound impact' in Russia sanctions
  • Prince Charles denies any wrongdoing over bags-of-cash claim
  • As summit host, Spain urges NATO to watch its southern flank
  • Iraqi caretaker PM in Iran to rekindle talks with Saudis
  • Italian nun slain in Haiti is hailed by pope as martyr
  • Survivors recount Mali's deadliest attack since coup
  • Israeli PM convenes Cabinet before parliament is dissolved
  • At least 20 dead in South African club; cause not yet known
  • Abortion protests continue after Supreme Court ends Roe v. Wade; Biden criticizes court’s ‘terrible decisions’

  • Your questions about the end of Roe, answered
  • GOP lawmaker calls Roe ruling ‘victory for white life’ as Trump rally cheers
  • A women’s clinic run by two generations of women braces for the post-Roe era
  • Seeking an abortion? Here’s how to avoid leaving a digital trail.
  • The trans Twitch star delivering news to a legion of LGBTQ teens
  • Biden 2024? America Needs to Know Now
  • Pro-Lifers Should Hold Off on Seeking National Abortion Ban
  • Biden’s Reversal on Gulf States Is the Wrong Kind of Realpolitik
  • Capping Russian Oil Prices Is Pure Fantasy
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • The pandemic is in a twilight zone. Enjoy it — but stay safe.
  • The racial ignorance of Virginia’s health czar
  • The problem with self-driving cars? Many don’t drive themselves.
  • The Supreme Court declares war on modern America
  • How the G-7 can tip the scales toward Ukraine
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Guardian: 26 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • England chasing 296 to win third Test against New Zealand on day four – live!
  • Glastonbury 2022: Sunday’s build up to Kendrick Lamar, Lorde and Pet Shop Boys – live
  • Russia-Ukraine war: one killed as Kyiv hit by missile strikes; G7 leaders gather in Germany – live updates
  • Concerns that India is ‘back door’ into Europe for Russian oil
  • Calls for investigation over deaths in Moroccan-Spanish border crossing
  • Boris Johnson warns of risk of fatigue in west’s support for Ukraine
  • Ten years that changed the face of women’s football
  • Ghislaine Maxwell put on suicide watch, but isn’t suicidal, ahead of sentencing
  • A classicist at Glastonbury: ‘Headbanging in raincoats? It’s as English as Gardeners’ Question Time’
  • Félix Auger-Aliassime: ‘I’ve shown great things. I’m closer and closer to my goal’
  • G7 grapples with packed agenda of world turned upside down
  • ‘I wish it hit my face’: 97 mph fastball breaks NL MVP Bryce Harper’s thumb
  • Roe v Wade, Donald Trump and Pride: the weekend’s best photos
  • Capitol attack hearings: if Republicans did nothing wrong, why were pardons sought?
  • Boris Johnson tries to calm Tory anger over his ‘third term’ remarks
  • US Soccer condemns supreme court abortion ruling as UWSNT beat Colombia
  • ‘Very, very modest’: Johnson vs Trudeau on whose private jet is smaller
  • Pablo Picasso: hidden photos reveal the artistic talent of his lover and muse
  • Tomb of Sand by Geetanjali Shree review – the timeless search to be seen
  • How I cope with feelings of envy by saying the Arabic word ‘mashallah’
  • Martin Bengtsson: football’s Swedish wonderkid whose dream died at Inter
  • Why is the US about to give away $52bn to corporations like Intel? | Robert Reich
  • History turns full circle as G7 alarm bells ring over energy once again | Larry Elliott
  • ‘Dom Phillips was natural storyteller – for us, he was always Uncle Dom’
  • Seventeen people found dead in South African nightclub
  • Curran Hatleberg’s humid, hallucinatory ​images of the deep south
  • Tory donor and Vladimir Putin associate facing forgery charges in Luxembourg
  • Bono tells of his secret half-brother and of family forgiveness
  • Martina Navratilova: ‘I try to do the right thing, not the popular thing’
  • Vaccinologist Sarah Gilbert: ‘We need to be better prepared for a new pandemic’
  • Tricks of their trade: meet the UK’s most unusual master crafters
  • ‘I wouldn’t have the money to pay a lawyer’: tenants left without means to sue rogue landlords
  • Labour has now claimed the centre ground – and has shown it can win | Keir Starmer
  • Zelensky: A Biography by Serhii Rudenko review – from voice of Paddington to global giant
  • Trajal Harrell: Porca Miseria review – a work of profound emotion
  • A third Tour de France would make Tadej Pogacar one of cycling’s true greats | William Fotheringham
  • US Covid-19 vaccine rollout for under-fives must overcome hesitancy
  • Elvis review – blistering, turbocharged chronicle of the King
  • ‘We jumped in the car as soon as we heard’: Afghans race to help amid wait for international aid
  • Emilia Clarke: ‘The best place in the world is backstage at a theatre’
  • Women must be allowed to defend abortion as a sex-based right | Sonia Sodha
  • Matteo Berrettini: ‘Winning Wimbledon? It sounds crazy but I know I can do it’
  • Timid west must draw a line in the sea and break Vladimir Putin’s criminal blockade | Simon Tisdall
  • Boris Johnson couldn’t bag a top job for Carrie but nepotism in politics is doing nicely | Catherine Bennett
  • Why did they wait? Uvalde anger grows over bungled police response
  • Conservatives’ alarm grows over threat of tactical voting in safe seats
  • ‘I feel joy and pride’: Tracee Ellis Ross on success, self-acceptance and her superstar mother
  • The big picture: dancing on the beach in the Camargue, 1957
  • NHS crisis caused by Tory underfunding not Covid, say doctors
  • Record number of LGBTQ+ candidates run for US Congress in wake of attacks
  • Sunday with Clara Amfo: ‘There’s nothing worse than the waste of a sunny day’
  • Pair green eyes and pink lips for a fun festival look | Funmi Fetto
  • Sri Lanka almost out of fuel, with no fresh supplies in sight
  • Prince Charles given €3m in cash in bags by Qatari politician, according to report
  • Trading in dreams: the US company that promised to make Australian children Disney stars – for a fee
  • ‘Walk around eccentrically with a large baguette’: seven ways to cope when everyone you know is on holiday in Europe
  • Greta Thunberg makes surprise appearance at Glastonbury festival
  • Campaigners welcome Sharon Stone’s decision to talk about her miscarriages
  • Roe v Wade: senators say Trump supreme court nominees misled them
  • Iran and US ready to restart talks on nuclear deal
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 26 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 26 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-26 14:00PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Rent control law fails to help Hong Kong’s poorest and leaves loopholes for landlords, advocacy group says

  • ‘We have nothing’ say survivors of deadly earthquake now facing disease outbreak
  • Netflix hit RRR featuring Jnr NTR shows India’s Telugu-language ‘Tollywood’ is no longer in Bollywood’s shadow
  • Ukraine war: Indonesia’s Jokowi says he will urge Putin to order immediate ceasefire during Moscow meeting
  • Pakistan loves chai so much, it may need to export green tea to fund its addiction
  • India’s cryptocurrency market was enjoying a ‘golden age’, but now it’s in a mess
  • As Australia eyes closer ties with Indonesia, Hazara refugees hope for immigration change
  • Singapore lures new wave of Chinese investors eyeing good schools, stability

  • Pride parades march on with new urgency amid calls for changes in law
  • Designer perfumes hit by war and weather as vital ingredient becomes ‘liquid gold’
  • Russia bombs central Kyiv as world leaders gather in Europe for G7 summit

  • Ghislaine Maxwell put on suicide watch ahead of sentencing, lawyer says
  • UK’s Boris Johnson seeks to stay in power until the mid-2030s
  • Russia intends to supply nuclear missile system to Belarus; Ukraine city of Sievierodonetsk falls
  • Greta Thunberg urges society to ‘set things right’ in speech at Glastonbury festival
  • FBI seizes Basquiat paintings from Florida exhibition amid doubts over authenticity
  • French government supports bill enshrining abortion rights in constitution
  • G7 leaders to agree on import ban on Russian gold as they broaden sanctions

  • Biden signs landmark gun control bill into law with bipartisan support ‘to save lives’
  • Why central bank digital currencies would mean the end of data privacy
  • Chinese fintech giant Ant Group appoints risk-control veteran as executive in consumer lending arm amid restructuring
  • As leaders squabble, a world economy in dire danger is in need of a peacemaking hero
  • Stablecoin implosion shows it has no role as a form of money, says Bank of International Settlements’ Asia chief
  • BEA’s new US$209 million southern China headquarters in Qianhai to serve as gateway to Greater Bay Area expansion, co-CEO Adrian Li says

  • Where do billionaires go on holiday? How Amazon founder Jeff Bezos, Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg, Apple’s Tim Cook and Virgin Group’s Richard Branson spend summers on yachts and in luxury mansions
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 26 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
